description_long: This is a roughly cuboidal stone with irregular, slightly rounded edges and a pitted, porous surface. The coloration is predominantly light gray with subtle variations, including some brownish and darker gray patches, suggesting natural mineral inclusions. The stone features several small holes and indentations, as well as a few larger, deeper crevices, giving it a weathered and natural appearance. Its surface is matte and rough, with no visible polish or artificial treatment. The stone appears solid and heavy, likely composed of volcanic or sedimentary rock. It would be commonly found as a barrier or obstacle in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped public spaces.
description: A roughly cuboidal, light gray stone with a pitted, porous, and weathered surface, featuring small holes and crevices. Its natural, matte finish and irregular shape suggest it is used as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows a mostly flat face with several small pits and a subtle, lighter patch near the center.
description_view_1: This side reveals a broader, more rounded face with two prominent horizontal crevices and a mix of gray and brownish tones.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a vertical crevice and a concentration of small holes, with a slightly more textured surface.
description_view_3: This view displays a relatively flat, less pitted face with a faint brownish discoloration and fewer visible crevices.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or grouped with similar stones as a barrier
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and mottling with some horizontal crevices and scattered mineral discoloration; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible signs of natural weathering, including small holes and surface discoloration, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces; may be partially surrounded by grass, soil, or mulch.
